studies.title: PDF Official title: Napping, Sleep, Cognitive Decline and Risk of Alzheimer ...
studies.abstract: Somryst delivers digital therapeutic content, and can be used on a mobile device, such as a smartphone or tablet. During each session, you will engage with the Somryst app (for about 30 minutes) and make daily entries into a sleep diary.
studies.brief_summary: Somryst delivers digital therapeutic content, and can be used on a mobile device, such as a smartphone or tablet. During each session, you will engage with the Somryst app (for about 30 minutes) and make daily entries into a sleep diary.

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions the Somryst app, describing its use and content, which aligns with the known description of the digital therapeutic product by Pear Therapeutics.

_classification.reason: The provided information lacks details on study design, such as randomization or observational data collection, and mentions 'study type: N/A', suggesting it is a protocol or description without results. This makes it ineligible as a primary RCT or RWE study.

studies.title: PDF Official title: Napping, Sleep, Cognitive Decline and Risk of Alzheimer ...
studies.abstract: Somryst is a 9-week program that delivers digital CBT-I therapeutic content, and can be used on a mobile device, such as a smartphone or tablet. CBT-I is a neurobehavioral treatment which focuses on addressing the maladaptive behaviors, routines, and dysfunctional thoughts that perpetuate sleep problems, regardless of the cause of sleep problems.
studies.brief_summary: Somryst is a 9-week program that delivers digital CBT-I therapeutic content, and can be used on a mobile device, such as a smartphone or tablet. CBT-I is a neurobehavioral treatment which focuses on addressing the maladaptive behaviors, routines, and dysfunctional thoughts that perpetuate sleep problems, regardless of the cause of sleep problems.

_verification.reason: The study specifically mentions Somryst, a 9-week program delivering digital CBT-I therapeutic content, which matches the description of the digital therapeutic product by Pear Therapeutics.

_classification.reason: The provided information lacks details on study design, such as randomization or observational data collection, and mentions 'Study type: N/A', suggesting it is not a primary study with results. It appears to be a protocol or description without results.
